candidate_problems.text: How can a system represent the originating contradiction of an explanation well enough to test whether varying a component reopens the original problem?
Are there explanations that are hard to vary for reasons other than genealogy from a specific problem-history, and if so how would this account distinguish them?
What formal or computational proxy could capture 'counterfactual necessity relative to the originating contradiction' for use in evaluating machine-generated explanations?
Can criticism over candidate texts recover load-bearing explanatory structure without explicit encoding of what prior ideas the explanation replaces?
When multiple historical contradictions converge on the same explanation, how should load-bearingness be attributed to individual components?
